# Reasoning Requirements Checklist: Reasoning Agents

**Purpose**: Validate that 007 reasoning requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and ready for implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-03-31
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are mode-specific requirements fully defined for `standard`, `devil_advocate`, and `comparison` without missing behavior paths? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK002 Are portfolio-context requirements explicit about when context must be included and when it must not be included? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK003 Are all mandatory Bookkeeper write-path requirements defined, including prior thesis lookup, snapshot, contradiction handling, and atomic persistence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006..FR-010]
- [x] CHK004 Are embedding requirements fully specified for both success and failure outcomes in KB persistence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK005 Are reporting requirements complete for labeling, fallback, chunking, and daily brief persistence across mission types? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012..FR-015]
- [x] CHK006 Are trade proposal requirements complete for context read, rationale constraints, approval status, warning text, and sell-position rejection? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016..FR-020]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is "schema-validated" defined with clear pass/fail criteria for malformed outputs and retry termination conditions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK008 Is "without direct external tool calls" explicit enough to prevent ambiguous interpretation of internal helper/tool abstractions? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK009 Is "high-severity" contradiction threshold definition explicitly documented or traceably referenced to avoid interpretation drift? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Gap]
- [x] CHK010 Is "platform length constraints" quantified with explicit target limits for chunking behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013, Gap]
- [x] CHK011 Is "exactly three sentences" rationale quality defined with objective sentence boundary rules for deterministic validation? [Measurability, Spec §FR-017]
- [x] CHK012 Is "non-held position" defined with unambiguous source-of-truth for holdings state at validation time? [Clarity, Spec §FR-020]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK013 Are the 007 role boundaries consistent across spec, plan, and contracts with no Analyst/Reporter/Trader responsibility overlap? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Scope Preservation, Contracts]
- [x] CHK014 Are contradiction requirements consistent between functional requirements and preserved design decisions (high-only alerting)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Spec §D-004]
- [x] CHK015 Are daily brief requirements consistent between reporting behavior and persistence expectations across artifacts? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015, Data Model §DailyBriefRecord, Contracts §Reporter]
- [x] CHK016 Are 006 compatibility requirements consistently represented in spec, plan, and contracts with matching field semantics (`confidence [0,1]`, `supportingHeadline`)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-021..FR-024, Contracts §Upstream Dependency]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Do acceptance scenarios cover all FR groups (Analyst, Bookkeeper, Reporter, Trader, compatibility) without orphan FRs? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-001..FR-024]
- [x] CHK018 Are success criteria objectively measurable and mapped to at least one requirement each? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001..SC-008]
- [x] CHK019 Are acceptance scenarios explicit about expected behavior under invalid model output and failed persistence paths? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, User Story 2, Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK020 Is there a requirement-level traceability scheme connecting FRs to planned tests/tasks for implementation gating? [Traceability, Gap]

## Scenario and Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are alternate flows for formatter fallback and chunked delivery requirements fully specified for ordering and retry expectations? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013..FR-014, Gap]
- [x] CHK022 Are exception-flow requirements defined for contradiction detection success combined with transaction failure rollback expectations? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK023 Are recovery requirements defined for temporary downstream channel failures (e.g., delayed delivery/retry ownership boundary)? [Recovery, Gap]
- [x] CHK024 Are boundary requirements defined for comparison mode input cardinality and invalid comparison payload cases? [Coverage, Spec §FR-002, Gap]

## Dependencies and Assumptions

- [x] CHK025 Are assumptions about 005 infrastructure availability converted into verifiable preconditions for implementation readiness? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK026 Are dependency boundaries with 008 explicitly non-conflicting for re-dispatch ownership and mission orchestration behavior? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Assumption]
- [x] CHK027 Are external dependency requirements (provider fallback availability and config ownership) explicit enough to avoid hidden runtime assumptions? [Dependency, Spec §FR-014, Assumption]
- [x] CHK028 Is change-management behavior defined for 006 contract evolution (who updates, where, and with what synchronization checks)? [Dependency, Spec §FR-024, Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add findings inline near each checklist item.
- Items are intentionally requirements-quality checks, not implementation tests.
- Completion evidence:
- `spec.md` now includes `Compatibility Acceptance Scenarios`, `Requirement Clarifications`, `Requirement Traceability`, `Contract Evolution Protocol`, and `Implementation Readiness Preconditions`.
- `contracts/reasoning-agents-contracts.md` now includes explicit invariants for comparison cardinality, Telegram 4096 chunk limit/order, non-fatal delivery failure ownership, and non-held position definition.
